zotero.abstractNote: Probing is a popular method for evaluating the linguistic knowledge encoded in representations learned by neural networks. However, the interpretation of probing results is often debated. In this paper, we propose a new perspective on probing: we frame it as a method for quantifying the inductive bias of a representation. We show that this perspective allows us to derive a formal relationship between probing performance and the sample complexity of learning a task from a representation. We validate our theoretical findings with experiments on a variety of tasks and representations, showing that our proposed measure of inductive bias is predictive of downstream performance.
zotero.conferenceName: Proceedings of the 60th Annual Meeting of the Association for Computational Linguistics (ACL)
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the 60th Annual Meeting of the Association for Computational Linguistics (ACL)
